text: Diego wants pi-intercom published to npm so The Last Harness can install it via npm instead of checking out this repository during installer runs; the motivation is faster TLH installation.

The release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) publishes with 'npm publish --provenance' and relies on npm OIDC Trusted Publishing, which the maintainer confirmed is already enabled for @diegopetrucci/pi-intercom on npmjs.com. There is intentionally no NPM_TOKEN secret. If publishing ever fails on auth, check the Trusted Publisher config on the npm package settings rather than adding a token. CI/release is a deliberate fork-only delta: upstream nicobailon/pi-intercom has no GitHub Actions at all.
